name: Qubes OS
description: Security-by-compartmentalization operating system using Xen hypervisor to isolate different activities in separate VMs. Applications run in disposable or persistent VMs that cannot access each other.
rationale: Qubes OS provides the strongest security isolation model of any desktop operating system. Each application domain (qube) is an isolated VM. A compromise in one qube — e.g., a malicious PDF exploit in a document viewer qube — cannot propagate to other qubes or the host. Whonix can be integrated as a network qube for anonymous Tor-routed traffic. Hardware requirements are significant (8GB+ RAM, VT-d support). Usability is substantially lower than mainstream desktop OSes. Security score reflects the isolation architecture; privacy score reflects that networking is still configurable and dependent on qube configuration.
